consumer_contexts: keyboard, mouse, gamepad, touch, gaze, hand, and controller input translation
gameplay, UI, tools, accessibility routes, and spatial interaction contexts
common_kernel: semantic intent, context, route, lifecycle, capability, and typed observation contracts
deterministic admission, collision resolution, policy evaluation, state transition, and diagnostics
engine and provider adapters that preserve semantic and identity boundaries
configurable_variation: bindings, interaction profiles, thresholds, hold and toggle policies, contexts, renderers, devices, and product handlers
excluded_project_specific: one game's commands, locomotion, grab rules, scene objects, interaction affordance art, and control layout
hard-coded vendor paths, device names, renderer components, singletons, scene search, and private assemblies
rationale: Action and intent mapping, context, routing, lifecycle, and rebinding recur across engines and input modalities; concrete gameplay commands, UI, XR techniques, renderers, and devices remain adapters or consumers.
non_claims: Admission does not prove a live input modality, XRI or OpenXR integration, accessibility outcome, comfort, renderer, vendor runtime, or device behavior.
